DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-07-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT LUCY PASTRANA'S GENERAL AND SPECIAL DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer to the second cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given 10 days leave, if he can do so in good faith, to plead facts that are not duplicative of Plaintiff's first cause of action to quiet title. Defendant's demurrer to the third cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to state a claim for ouster. (Complaint, 12, 15, 16, 24; see also Zaslow v. Kroenert (1946) 29 Cal.2d 541, 548; Kapner v. Meadowlark Ranch Ass'n (2004) 116 Cal.App.4th 1182, 1189.) Defendant's demurrer for uncertainty is overruled as it is not dispositive of Plaintiff's cause of action for partition. (See Khoury v. Maly's of Calif., Inc., 14 CA4th 612, 617 (cited in Cal. Prac. Guide Civ. Pro. Before Trial Ch. 7(I)-A.)) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-09-2013 Continued To Nov-07-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No Appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LUCY PASTRANA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Plaintiff's Peremptory Challenge having been accepted by the Honorable Ronald Evans Quidachay, this motion is to be heard in Department 302 on November 7, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. per the order of the presiding judge. All future motions in this case to be noticed in Department 302. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk J. Rumsey, Not Reported. (501)
